description: Fix profile picture upload failures â€” improve client error surfacing, fix HEIC decoding in Alpine Docker, and add a diagnostics endpoint for storage and sharp health

userStories.title: Surface server error messages in client upload function
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a specific error message when my upload fails so I know whether to retry, use a different image, or report a bug.
acceptanceCriteria: In `apps/web/src/lib/api.ts`, update the `apiUploadFile()` function to read the JSON response body for ALL non-OK status codes â€” not just 400 and 413
Replace the generic `if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Upload failed. Please try again.')` with logic that reads `await res.json()` and throws the server's `error` field if present
For 500 responses, the thrown error message should come from the server body (e.g., 'Image processing failed. Please try a different image.')
For 503 responses, the thrown error message should come from the server body (e.g., 'Storage service unavailable. Please try again.')
If the response body cannot be parsed as JSON, fall back to a category-specific message based on status code: 5xx â†’ 'Upload service error. Please try again.'
Existing 413 and 400 handling remain unchanged
Existing timeout and network error handling remain unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a quick win that makes debugging easier. The server already returns specific error messages in the JSON body for 500 and 503 status codes, but the client currently ignores them.

userStories.title: Add HEIC codec support to Alpine Docker image
userStories.description: As an iPhone user, I want to upload HEIC photos without them failing at the image processing step.
acceptanceCriteria: In `services/api/Dockerfile`, add `RUN apk add --no-cache libde265` to the runtime stage (the second FROM block, after `FROM node:24-alpine`) to provide the HEIC decoder

If `libde265` is not available in Alpine's repos or does not integrate with sharp's bundled libvips, switch BOTH stages of the Dockerfile from `node:24-alpine` to `node:24-slim` (Debian-based) and use `RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends libde265-0 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` instead
After the fix, running `docker build -f services/api/Dockerfile -t burnbuddy-api-test .` succeeds
After the fix, running `docker run --rm burnbuddy-api-test node -e "console.log(require('sharp').format.heif)"` shows `.heic` in `fileSuffix` OR buffer input is true (confirming HEIC decode support)
Existing JPEG/PNG/WebP processing is not affected (no regression)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: sharp 0.34.5 bundles libvips with libheif, but the Alpine prebuilt binary only includes the AVIF codec (libaom). HEIC requires the libde265 codec. sharp.format.heif.input.fileSuffix currently only shows ['.avif'], not '.heic'/'.heif'. The preferred approach is adding libde265 to Alpine; if that doesn't work with the bundled libvips, fall back to a Debian base image.

userStories.title: Create diagnostics route for storage and sharp health
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a diagnostics endpoint that reports the health of storage and image processing so I can detect issues proactively.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a new file `services/api/src/routes/diagnostics.ts` with a default-exported Express router
Add `GET /` route (will be mounted at `/diagnostics`) protected by `requireAuth` middleware from `../middleware/auth`

Response JSON includes `sharp` object with: `version` (string from `sharp.versions.sharp`), `heifSupport` (boolean from `sharp.format.heif.input.buffer`), `heifFileSuffixes` (array from `sharp.format.heif.input.fileSuffix`)

Response JSON includes `storage` object with: `bucketName` (string from `FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET` env var or the default), `bucketExists` (boolean from `bucket.exists()` call), `credentialsPresent` (boolean â€” true if `FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON` env var is set and non-empty, does NOT leak the actual value)
If any check throws an error, catch it and include an `error` field in that section instead of crashing (e.g., `{ storage: { error: 'Failed to check bucket' } }`)
Register the router in `services/api/src/index.ts` with `app.use('/diagnostics', diagnosticsRouter)` â€” import it alongside existing route imports
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use getStorageBucket() from lib/storage.ts for the bucket check. Import sharp for version/format info. The endpoint helps debug future storage or image processing issues without needing shell access to the container.

userStories.title: Add unit test for diagnostics endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want test coverage for the diagnostics endpoint so regressions are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `services/api/src/routes/diagnostics.test.ts` following the existing test pattern: use `vi.hoisted` + `vi.mock` to stub firebase-admin, firestore, storage, and logger
Mock `sharp` to return a fake `versions` object and `format.heif` object
Mock `getStorageBucket` to return a mock bucket with a `.exists()` method
Test: `GET /diagnostics` returns 401 when unauthenticated
Test: `GET /diagnostics` returns 200 with `{ sharp: { version, heifSupport, heifFileSuffixes }, storage: { bucketName, bucketExists, credentialsPresent } }` shape when authenticated
Test: endpoint returns partial results with error field when `bucket.exists()` throws
Tests pass (`cd services/api && yarn test`)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact mock pattern from services/api/src/routes/users-profile-picture.test.ts â€” use vi.hoisted for mock functions, vi.mock for module replacements, import router after mocks, buildApp() helper, supertest for HTTP assertions.
